William Neumann.review_text: Honestly Neuman makes me question MIS as a major. The class doesn't actually teach you anything useful at all besides maybe how to use Microsoft excel. His tests are a regurgitated mess of questions that for about 75% of them the only way you would get them right is if you memorized that exact question from a previous exam that was posted online.

Lectures are useless apart from attendance points. The inclusive E-book is a hassle. If only the lectures pertained more to the book material rather than reviewing that we have homework. The information on D2L is a total mess compared with other classes. Easy class but it needs help, whats the point of a professor if we depend on the book to learn.
Very nice guy, just never finishes his lectures before class ends

I like Dr. Neumann tons more than Patton. The class is basically a total lecture with tons of quizzes and the projects are annoyingly long, but Neumann himself is really nice and cool. I probably would like the class better if it didn't have 570 or so students and could ask him more specific questions and follow up better.

The lectures are so boring but he is a good teacher. But overall the class is fair. You'll get back what you put in. REVIEW OLD EXAMS. I can not stress that enough because they are almost exactly the same as the test you will take.
Boring doesn't even begin to describe this guy. As long as you can make it through the lectures without peeling your eyes out you may be able to do well in this class.

As long as you're not sitting on your computer doing other stuff it's fairly easy to absorb the information. There's a 1-unit online lab as well, but it's very structured and as long as you have the Excel Bible you'll be able to figure it all out. never once used the lecture text book. Honors section makes the class 10 times easier.

This man is a piece a work. We took our finals last week. He hasn't posted  official final grades . It's December 24th. Simply unacceptable. If we turned in our assignments this late..... We wouldn't.

I did not enjoy this class at ALL. There is so much work over the course of the semester close to 1000 points but business majors have no choice in taking it. very long boring lectures, and tests are pretty hard but short. There are four massive projects that take hours to do and is nothing but busy work and excel. get ready to work.

Honestly, Dr. Neumann was the best out of the different MIS professors. He at least was understandable with the material. The class overall is extremely easy; you get 5 easy points just for attending lecture and filling out the ICQ. The majority of the assignments are extremely easy; the hardest thing is the projects.

You don't learn much from the class because of the amount of information. It's open-note and open-web so the mini-quizzes and exams are simple. The ABPs (3 heavily-graded projects throughout the semester) are not difficult, especially if you take advantage of the two weeks given. Each one takes less time as you get used to the layout. Honest prof.

Prof. Neumann, for the lectures he was present at, was a very average, well-spoken individual, but the lecture content was relatively boring. The MIS111 class is pretty useless and easy, but does require some effort, especially for the three ABPs (all of which are completable in a day). However, the amount of (useless) content is a bit ridiculous.

If you do the work in this class it is one of the easier classes to get a A-B in. There are a lot of assignments but they take 5 minutes to do. The projects are more lengthy but you have so much time to do it the only people who really struggle waited until they had a day left. Unfortunately there is so much info the class is meant be memorized.
He is kind, calm, and funny. He goes directly to the point he makes, but you have to follow, and he will not return to a given point.
Dr Neumann is really nice and really funny. He can be really good with doing the lectures and should be accessible outside of class.

Dr. Neumann is a great professor. He is clearly knowledgable and passionate about the MIS field and is very approachable. 111 material is nothing too difficult so long as you do the readings and attend lecture. 112 material is also pretty accessible.

Of the two professors that teach MIS111, Dr. Neumann is the better of the two. He is passionate about what he teaches, and understands that the content may be boring to the students. Honestly an easy class. Just make sure you do all the work and you'll get an easy A.

By far the better of the two professors for this class. He obviously cares a lot about MIS and if this lecture wasn't so huge I probably would have liked him more. Class itself is pretty easy, just show up and do the ICQs, study the old exams they post, start the projects early and you'll be fine.
Neumann is a GEM!!!! Gotta respect the cute ties & adorable stories! class is easy.
Lots of lectures in class about seemingly random, insignificant things but they always end up on the exams.

MIS is a class you are going to have a lot of work in so know that from the start. Neumann knows the material isnt the most interesting, but he trys to make lectures bearable. Assignments are all very easy if you start ahead of time and follow the directions. Only difficult thing about the class is the exams.

Excellent professor with extremely engaging lectures, even with rather boring material. The course itself was straightforward and even easy, and Dr. Neumann made the lectures worth attending. Strongly recommend him and the course.

MIS 111 isn't a hard class, just keep up with the readings, PARs, and homework and you'll pass. Dr. Neumann is a really boring teacher, better than the other professor for the course. Just come to class and do the homework and you'll be fine.

You have to take him for MIS111 but he's a nice and caring guy. The lecture material is boring, and the class is an easy A if you do the homework and assignments (lots of work), but he works with what he's got.

Dr. Neumann makes MIS111 bearable. He is very kind and helpful with any questions, and makes the topics easy to understand in his lectures. The material isn't particularly interesting, but the class itself is very manageable. Expect homework due before each class, three projects, three exams, and a final. Attendance is done through clickers.
Make sure you stay on top of your PARs and ABPs and youll be fine

Super nice guy. The lectures aren't the most interesting topic ever, but are essential for the passing the class. Review old tests for the exam; they are very much the same. He uses clickers, and puts pretty much all of his lectures online.

This is really the most straight forward class in existence. Don't listen to anyone saying this class is by any means difficult. You get exactly what you put in, literally just don't be a miserable student, and you have an A

Ok professor. Overexplains everything, and is not all that interesting to listen to. He is in charge of the honors section, and we never got our stuff on time, so get ready to be frustrated (it took 3 months to get our 1st projet grades).  Class is super easy, need to show up for clicker points, but don't have to pay attention.

The Applied Business Projects for this class are absolutely ridiculous. By far the most time consuming assignments I've ever had to do and so boring it's like my brain is being strangled. If it weren't for the applied business projects this course would be very manageable. A lot of people say the lectures are boring but I think he does a great job.
I love the teacher not so much the class

The weekly homework is easy points. There are a lot of projects though but as long as you follow the directions exactly they are easy. Also going to flex labs is almost always a must for the projects, which is quite tedious. Attendance is also taken everyday along with daily quizzes.

Dr. Neumann is a great guy. He really manages a ton of students well and tries to help whenever possible. MIS 111 is a weed out class. The projects are time consuming but graded easily, go to the flex labs for everything. The tests are easy if you study well. If you can't get at least a B in this class you probably are a frat boy w/ a negative IQ.
He never updates grades so you never know what you have in the class. He has the most boring lectures ever too.

Have to take this class for business. Good luck. Group up with people for projects and work together. HIGHLY RECCOMMEND GETTING A PC LAPTOP. You have to click in for attendance. You can miss 7 classes which is nice. Lots of projects and very uninteresting information

Definitely go to class to earn clicker questions. Case analysisABP projects are hard but using the excel bible helps a little. Study the past exams and you'll do good on the tests. Long lectures but a great teacher, however a really boring class

There were 2 parts to this class. The In-Class section met up 2 times a week for a 1 hour 15 minute lecture that can get very boring. These are necessary as you get clicker points. There is also the Online section where we had many of the tests and assignments. Class was pretty easy but could get boring. Good overall.
Tons of busy work, and pointless business projects, professor is also often hard to reach for help or explanation.

Easiest class of my life. Drops so much! Take as honors!!!! It is easier projects and no final, just a half hour round table discussion on weeks there is no test. Class is still easy not as honors though.

Pretty easy class if you go. Attendance is a big part of your grade. There are a total of 4 projects, but they are simple if you attend the flex labs. There are 4 tests, with answers clearly given, but only 3 count as part of your grade. Neumann also has pretty cool ties.

Neumann isn't a bad guy, with 500 students per class he does his best to ensure you have the material you need to succeed, be aware he takes 3 weeks to grade an exam. As long as you show up and pay attention you'll get the material. The only challenging part is ABPs. There is a lot of use of Excel, he will help you if you talk with him in person.

Class is not an easy A like everyone says it is. You have multiple assignments every class which arent hard but are easy to forget every now and then which hurts your grade. The projects are extremely time consuming and I learned almost nothing from this class.

This course in general is awful. I don't ever understand what is going on. Prof spends 30 mins of his class just for announcements, keep repeating the same thing and hard teaches any content. If this class wasn't mandatory, I would have dropped it for sure.

Overall this professor was my favorite out of the other 4 that co-taught this class. In terms of material, this class can be tedious, the reading was tough especially since you needed to complete the reading, watch the videos and take the quiz before class. The projects also didn't help, one thing I definitely learned is managing my time. Not bad!

MIS111 is a decent intro class, though the projects can be tedious. Luckily, resources like unstuckstudy ai and office hours help a lot. Lectures are chill, and the quizzes/exams are straightforward if you stay on top of things.

For business, this is a good introduction class. The lectures are very chill and low stakes, and so are the quizzes. The projects can be time consuming, but they tell you exactly what they want so if you follow through on it, you'll get a good grade. MIS 112 is pretty similar and chill as well. You'll need this class for first year, it'll be good

MIS 111 was pretty easy, although the Applied Business Projects are a pain in the butt. Best part is that attendance is 100% optional, and all of the classes are filmed on Zoom so if you want to hear the lectures, you can just sit in your dorm bed!
Honestly, this class is easiest if you're in Greek life/ or if you can get in a group chat. For projects, the directions are really hard to understand but Dr. Neumann can help if you go to class.

He's a fine professor & he cares about his students HOWEVER.. the projects in this class are ridiculously hard. As a first semester freshman with zero prior excel knowledge I had to get SO much help with them. They were seriously so tedious & took not hours, DAYS to do. I ended with a B in the class but it wasn't easy for me! Made me switch majors.
The assignments open for a day then close so its very easy to miss them, they also open at 6 pm which was incredibly annoying. The projects get graded harshly and late penalty seems extreme.

Dr. Neumann and the other MIS 111/112 instructors were clear with grading/assignments and care for the students. There are two quizzes per class but once in a routine it was pretty manageable. Exams are purely based off the textbook and lectures. The three projects were long and worth a lot of points so just start reasonably early.

Dr. Neumann and the other MIS 111/112 instructors were very accommodating throughout the semester and did not leave any questions unanswered. This class is very lecture heavy but it is very possible to get an A if you do all the PARs and KCs as well as read the lectures. This course was a good introduction into Eller.

Professor Neumann is very kind and responds to emails quickly. You can tell he always tries his best to make the class worthwhile. The projects are simple, but not related to what is taught in class. Don't be discouraged if you get a point or two off on anything cause there's a lot of EC, grade appeals, and partial credit to make up for it :)

This course gives lots of work, with 2 quizzes usually per class as well as lengthy projects and big exams. However, Dr. Neumann does what he can to make it all manageable. His lectures were pretty good with little personal stories being added but overall he explained the concepts pretty well.

This class is super obnoxious and outdated, but Dr. Neumann does this best to make it entertaining and feel more relevant than it really is. Super sweet and caring, always smiling. Overall, it's easy enough to pass, though the big projects don't seem to relate and aren't really taught in class. Class itself is useless, but profs are nice.

Dr. Neumann is the purest nicest guy on Earth. He's always smiling and super sweet. Emails are answered super quickly. There are like 6 quizzes a week but all of the answers are either on the slides or online. There are three huge projects that are kinda hard. So I would start them as early as you can. Overall good class and I love Dr. Neumann

This class is super easy, all the homeworks and exam answers were easily found online. The three projects you have to do throughout the semester are very easy, but super time consuming. There were just like little assignments due every 2 days or so that were annoying. Also im not exactly sure what this class is trying to teach, but its easy

MIS 111 is a tedious class that covers a wide range of seemingly unrelated topics. Dr. Neumann was cheerful and attempted to engage the class during lectures; replies to emails. All homework and quizzes are all open note and open web. The three projects are time-consuming, but the grading criteria are clear. Overall, an easy, VERY tedious class.

Professor Neumann was a really overall good teacher. While I think that MIS can definitely be boring, he definitely tried to make it interesting. Professor was very easy to communicate through emails when you had questions. I would definitely recommend Professor Neumann

Neumann was fine, but the class itself is pretty questionable. Powerpoint presentations look like they were made 20 years ago and the content was hyperspecific all the time when it didn't need to be (this is an introductory class covering a huge number of topics). My favorite thing about this class was the open-note nature of all assessments.

Dr. Neumann is a good professor. He's very knowledgeable on the lecture topics, responds relatively quick to emails, and is always willing to help. MIS111 as class is pretty boring and the homework is tedious, though pretty easy. Put in a decent amount of effort and you'll easily pass with an A. Google will be your best friend in this class.

I'm not sure why people rate this class so bad. There are lots of quizzes (only 5 questions), but if you keep up with them you will be fine. The projects are time consuming, but the rubrics go SO in depth that it should be almost impossible to do bad on them. Material can be boring, but overall not a bad professor or class.
Literally went to class one time and ended with a 97. Attendance does not matter at all. Easiest class ever
Not hard if you do the readings and watch the lectures. Dr. Neumann was not the most engaging during lectures but gave good information and was very helpful and kind.
If you do the assignments you will get an A, simple as that.

Dr. Neumann gives a lot of busy work for no reason. You will have a project, or an exam due every week. The lectures are hard to follow and are really boring. If you dont have experience in excel this class will be difficult. Try not to get this professor.

This is a very easy class if you just do the work. There are about 6 quizzes per week but answers are usually on quizlet (5 questions each, no time limit, 2 attempts). There are 6 projects, which are time-consuming but pretty straightforward. Attendance wasn't mandatory (lectures posted on D2L). There were 3 exams, with 50 questions in 50 minutes.

Don't let the other reviews scare you because this class wasn't that bad. There are a lot of projects that are time-consuming, but as long as you read the instructions and follow them, you will get a 100% on all of them. Additionally, there are multiple quizzes per week, but they're easy.

This Professor is one of the worst I have ever had. I have never seen a teacher so boring and such a tough grader. His projects are very hard if you dont have a slight experience about what this class is. Get ready to do 6 quizzes a week, Hard projects and a boring life sucking class. This class is very time consuming.

MIS111 is a very manageable course if you do the work. There are 6 quizzes a week (7 if you include MIS112 quiz) and they are pretty easy. Each course has 3 major projects that mainly make up your grade. If you do your best it is easy to get 100% on them, but they are time-consuming. Going to zoom class is really helpful, but not mandatory.

Dr. Neumann is a pretty bad prof (I've had worse tho), but his course is the worst course I've ever taken. MIS 111 and 112 are the absolute worst and should be removed. Get ready for hours of useless, incredibly boring material. That is my reasoning for these low ratings.

Pretty difficult course, but easy to BS. Dr. Patton was a pleasing guy to listen to and the class was just super chill. Besides the exams and terrible Applied Business Projects (those literally kill you unless you do good on them), he is a good professor! He is very intrigued by what he teaches which makes paying attention easier.

Even though this class is almost exclusively lectures, Neumann does an astounding job of being professional and articulate about MIS. Extremely knowledgeable about his content matter and tells you exactly what you need to do to get an A. Very enjoyable class and a very enjoyable professor.

Expectations on students are clear. Assignments are relevant to learned information. Midterms pull directly from quizzes that are taken in class and assigned as HW. Everything is predictable. You just need to pay attention and keep up with the homework. A very intelligent person who enjoys sharing his knowledge and does so wonderfully.

Dr. Neumann is passionate about what he teaches and though the class can be boring at times, Dr. Neumann's attitude and energy each time he lectured was awesome. The hardest thing about this class is that it's time demanding. Be ready to work. Do your PAR's, ABP's, and participate in class.

Awesome teacher, super easy class! Don't listen to anyone that says otherwise. Attend lectures and get those clicker points. Easy assignments just follow the instructions, they are so direct it is like he is answering it for you. Review old exams before the tests. If you have trouble with ABPs or CAs go to the flexlab it helps a lot!

You can miss up to 9 classes w/o penalty and Professor Neumann drops the lowest module exam out of 3 module exams during the semester. There is 4-5 clicker questions/lecture and attendance is taken by clickers. ABP Projects are not hard, just time consuming, instructions are very clear cut in D2L. Lectures are somewhat dull.
Do your APBs, study for the tests, and turn everything in on time for the love of all that is holy. This class was mediocre. Turn things in on time and attend lectures, you will pass the class

The clicker questions can be annoying, but you can miss 9 days of the class. He teaches with another professor, but he is the better of the pair. He is more lenient with grades and extreme circumstances. A little theatrical but it makes this boring subject somewhat interesting.

Dr. Neumann is a very well respected MIS and Eller professor and also an avid tie wearer and is very nice in person but in class might seem very monotone and boring. He does include his old fashion humour. He repeats everything!!! Class is annoying but very easy, you'd had to be a complete moron to not pass the class since he repeats everything.
Difficult class but if you study hard then it is possible to get an A. they drop the lowest test score out of 3 tests so that helps.

Dr. Neumann is a very fair professor. His lectures are very boring but it's hard to blame him because the class itself is very boring. However a lot of the content on tests are not mentioned by him at all. He posts old test so make sure you read those because 90% of the problems come straight from that. Pretty easy class. Lots of free points.

Attendance is mandatory and his lectures will always be included with the exams. Neumann is a mixed bag, he can be funny, inspirational, and so on. Just do not interrupt him. Use the book code and decline the offer for the book. You will still have access and not pay.

He's a nice guy, and you can tell he loves his job, but his lectures are so boring and not engaging at all. He speaks to you instead of discussing, and it sucks, because the information he talks about needs to be explained. Pay attention during syllabus week or you'll be hurting.
There are many quizzes and projects, but they are all do-able. The prof explains and presents the material very well. Material is posted online. I have no complaints for this class!
Lots of work but the projects aren't too hard, just time consuming. Lot's of online quizzes. The reason for a lower grade was because of the tests.
I really enjoyed this class. I wasn't very interested in the material but he made me want to learn more. He can be strict sometimes, but he is very informative and you will learn a lot.

I'm not entirely a fan of MIS, I just had to take MIS111 like all pre-business majors. the class was co-taught by Neumann and another professor... least to say Neumann would be who I would want to be lecturing that day. He also has awesome neck ties that correlate with what he's teaching that day which always made me happy.

This class is mandatory for business and the professor is great but the class itself is so confusing. There is a lot of stuff you have to figure out on your own so I highly recommend finding a good study group and thoroughly reading the directions. STUDY for the test too. the answers are all in past tests and it is a huge part of your grade.
This dude sucks. Most boring professor of all time, he looks like colonel sanders. Very vague and its mostly you teaching yourself.

Professor Neumann was such a great professor! He was passionate about what he taught, although the class was still boring. From taking this class last semester, the exams were easy; you just needed to study the previous exams and the only hard part about this course are the ABP's and Case Analysis. FlexLab was a great resource to go to for help!
I like this class and would want to take it again.  FlexLab is a really useful tool to get help for projects.

MIS 111 is an easy A. There are 3 lengthy projects, but they are graded easily. There are 3 exams and the final, the lowest exam grade is dropped. Study the previous exams and the powerpoints. Sometimes the lectures can be boring, but if you take notes the class will go by quicker.

William is an extremely boring lecturer, but some of the info is kind of interesting. The projects are really difficult so try to get with someone who's taken the class before. Clicker questions every day so you can't skip class. He posts previous exams online so make sure to really study those and your notes, and you'll get an A on the exams.

Dr. Neumann was pretty good. I liked him a lot more than Dr. Patton who co-taught MIS 111 with Neumann. He's a great guy and respects you if you respect him. Study the tests from previous years and start the applied business projects ahead of time and you'll get an A in his class. Unlike Patton, he really seems to care about his students.

Dr. Neumann is a good guy, but because the class is so big it is hard to get that. He is tri-toned (instead of monotone). You will get the grade you work for. Go to flex labs, do the extra credit. Buy the Bible but do not buy the textbook access code because you probably won't use it. All of the classes are posted online if you need to miss.
He is a great professor and a super wise man. You can miss up to 7 classes, but doing so isn't the best choice. Super easy final.

Easy class if you pay attention, and review slides. Neumann literally uses questions for the three mid-terms from old exams which he gives you. Final consists of questions from 3 mid terms and 1/2 new qs. Really easy to get A. Lectures can become very boring. Business projects are easy and self explanatory - a lot of extra credit opp. very easy A.

Overall, the class wasn't hard, but the lectures were LONG and BORING. I didn't miss a class at all the entire semester and I found it pretty easy. Most likely would have gotten an A if I had studied previous exams. The final was easy, but I'm glad the class is over.

Neuman can be boring but if you go to class and study a little you should get an A easily. Study previous exams word for word and you should be good to go on the tests. Do assignments on time because he gives extra credit if you hand it in on time.
He is the most BORING lecturer ever. I literally learned nothing from this guy. This class just sucks and he doesn't make it any better.

Easy B, easy A if are a good student. Projects aren't as hard as they look because they grade easy. I haven't finished the class yet but i think I'm going to get an A. drop one test, you can miss 7 classes, drop a few other things too. lenient teachers. Boring, long, but mandatory for business students. book mandatory
The class can be very long and boring but some information is important. Tests are easy if you study and the professor can get a little rambley.

Lectures are boring and the topics are random but a lot of them are useful. Projects are not difficult at all, just really time-consuming. You will most likely do well on tests if you study the previous years' tests word by word. No textbook needed but if you are also taking the 1 credit lab class you need the Excel Bible.

The only reason that you would not pass MIS 111 is if you do not go to class and do not do homework. He wants you to do well and makes it easy if you just try. Look at the practice test questions he gives you, study them, they help

Regardless what anyone says, MIS 111 is an easy and kind of fun class. You learn a bunch of random useful stuff that will help you in your everyday life, and the material is very straightforward. The projects and tests kinda suck but if you put in the time you'll get an A no doubt

No homework, it's assigned but you don't really need to do it. Three test throughout the year, studymemorize the old exams and you'll be fine. Turn in ABP's early because that extra credit can help. There's clicker attendance and a sign-in sheet at the end for 10 random names, but he lets you miss some classes. Google the knowledge check answers

Talks the whole time. Big on clickers. I don't understand him at all while he talks, but for tests I just go over his notes he posts for each class and look at old exams he posts and do okay. ABPs are a pain in the butt and kinda hard. Knowledge checks are easy to look up online if you just copy and paste questions into google. Cool guy!
There is no clear direction to the class

I loved professor neumann! All the previous exams were posted online and about 90% of the questions were taken from them. It takes a while to review all of them, but it's worth it to get a good grade. Also, if you're an honors student, take the honors section!! it's about 200x's easier. You don't have as many projects and when you do they're EASY

Honestly people are being really damatic. The class is really not that hard, if you just pay attenting to the main points of the lecture and work with a friend to do the projects you will be fine. The tests are not that hard, just study the old tests he provides you with. SO every one can calm down because you will be just fine.

If this class isn't required for your major and you are taking it then something is wrong with you. lectures are beyond boring and tests are almost impossible to study for! So much busy work that will stress you out like no other but its always nice to know that 2000 kids just like you are going through the same problems. Do all the work on time.

Most boring class ever. Learned nothing from it. You have to do a ABP project every other week and then have a CA project the weeks you don't have a ABP due. Those projects take FOREVER and you learn nothing from it. They have nothing to do with the class. Definitely take the make up! DO NOT MISS THAT OPPORTUNITY.

Class is pretty easy but way to time consuming for what it is. The CAs and ABPs take FOREVER, are not really related to the class, and it is hard to understand them without asking peers for help. Study for tests you'll do fine, and take the makeup (easiest test of my life). Show up for AP points, but you don't have to listen (internet is better)

Went to class every day spent all of my time on facebook, instagram, twitter, youtube, and anything BUT this class. Tests are ok if you study well, extra credit is offered (DO it, you will need it), the applied business projects have nothing to do with the class so you're on your own. Make friends with a lot of business students, you will need them

This class isn't hard, just a lot of work. Go to classes to get clicker points, which is your participation grade. Projects are time consuming, I recommend going to flex labs for additional help. This class is a PREREQ for business and Eller, so don't slack off.

Attending class is mandatory. Tests are easy if you take some time to study. Most questions are from previous exams that he posts. Projects are unclear but go to flexlabs to get help and you will do fine. Time consuming class but not terribly hard.
Pretty easy class if you attend class, keep up with the projects and knowledge checks. Gives extra credit opportunities and makeup exam.

It's unfortunate for Dr. Neumann (and others) when students talk when he is talking. It forces him to stop talking completely and delay the class. The class is easy if the reading is done and you show up for attendance credit. Exams are also pretty easy if you study beforehand. I just wish there was more discipline for chatty people.
Hes got the cutest southern accent and very articulate so the lecture isnt awful. Material isnt difficult, just a lot of memorizing. Need to take for business major.
Big class so he does his best. The homework instructions are bland but the tasks are easy once understood.
up to date content, very smart man.  lot of work but a lot learned.
Very easy class, but boring. Do the ABPs and do decent on the tests and you'll get a A.

The class is interesting if you're a marketing major. The lecture is boring upside is that there is no discussion section to attend. The only challenging thing is the use of Excel. I asked him where I could find information on how to do a specific function and he replied "determining to isolate the last two digits is part of the assignment"

I thought he was a cool guy, but the courseload is VERY rigorous. It gave me a really useful skill to take away from the class (Excel knowledge) which is important because not a lot of people know Excel.
in terms of the process

One of the worst professors I've had at the U of A -- and that's saying a lot. Instructions are unclear, wastes time in class, terrible attendance policy, overly obsessed with technology, ignores emails, most boring lectures ever, business projects are time consuming and unclear. However, he does grade pretty easiliy -- only good quality he has.

Class is super easy and you don't really have to pay attention. Just read all case studies he assigns and you should be good on the tests. Make sure you go to class and do the projects. Final is not mandatory so it is super nice if you do well enough on everything else.

BUSY WORK. Easy, but time consuming. Slides and lecture recordings are online, yet attendance is mandatory. In the MIS111/111L combo, every week either a huge project or a paper is due (it alternates). Online quizzes 3 times/week, clicker points in class, 3 exams, optional final. Those good at Excel will have a much easier time than the rest of us!

The class is mostly projects which are graded on effort. If you do all of them, you are pretty much guaranteed an A. The lectures can be pretty boring, but the guest lectures are cool. He seems mean when he's talking in front of everyone, but if you talk to him in a small group, he's really nice and understanding.
BORING! Half the time his slides are either wrong or outdated, and the other half he has guest lecturers. Spends the first 20 min of every class recapping the upcoming hw assignment and tests.

Pay attention in class, do the readings, and do the assignments, easy A. The tests are hard, but if you do well with everything else, you can still easily get an A. Seriously they grade the projects based on effort.

This class was a lot of work. There was a project every week, readings, homework, and knowledge checks after each class. I don't mind a lot of work, but I did more work in this class than my other 4 classes all combined. I learned a lot about excel and enjoyed the guest speakers... Overall, do the work, and you will pass
Use google, and ctrl+f! They'll become your best friends, pretty easy A with some effort, a B if you just show up and take some notes.

I did not learn a single thing in this class besides not to click on links in emails. As long as you get the Socratic app to do in class quizzes and at home quizzes you should be fine. To study for tests just memorize all of the quiz questions (I made quizlets and got As and Bs on my tests although the average is a C). Projects are easy.

Dr. Patton knows the material very well. However, he is very monotone and goes over the material pretty quickly. He definitely gets irritated with the class at times and will call someone out if they have headphones in.

Class in itself was very easy, show up, do the clickers, review previous exams, do the ABP's and get an A. Patton is very monotone which can get quite mundane, but still an easy class. Doesn't curve grades so don't slack off and do the work and you'll get the A you deserve.

Great Professor, cares about what he teaches and very helpful. The class is easy just do your assignments on time and go to class to get attendance points. I went to the office hours once and he was really helpful and patient and made everything I did not understand clear. Highly recommended.
He makes the lecture interesting and he's good at explaining things as well.

Mark seemed like he never wanted to be teaching. I get that Mis111 is a huge class (500 or so) but he was very uninspiring. Class is an easy A, and you have to take him, but he is not a very fun teacher or person.

MIS111 is not difficult. All expectations are very clear, and the grading is down to a science. Expect a homework assignment due before every class, three projects, three module exams, and a final. The material was not particularly interesting, but it is not difficult to understand either. Attendance is done through clicker sign in.
Monotonous, but got the information across and had a clear outline of what was needed for the class

Slightly prefer Patton to Nuemann. He injects a bit more personality into the otherwise dry lectures and was always nice when addressing issues during office hours. MIS 111 can feel like a very pointless class at times because of all the different topics covered but it's ridiculously simple and easy from quizzes to tests to projects. Easy A.

This class is super obnoxious and outdated, but Dr. Patton tries to make it somewhat bearable. Can be monotone, but he's fairly easy to work with. Overall, it's easy enough to pass (google's your friend), though the big projects don't seem to relate and aren't really taught in class. Class itself is useless, but profs are nice enough.
Pointless class, nothing useful is taught and the professors teach as if they are teaching 6 year olds. No need to come to class.

MIS111 is a very easy class if you put in a little effort. Almost all of the answers to the quizzes are on Quizlet. There is usually some reading, a topic overview lecture, and a 5 question quiz before class, and then another 5 question quiz after the in-person lecture. No need to show up to class and Dr. Patton was a good teacher.

This is a very easy class if you just do the work. There are about 6 quizzes per week but answers are usually on quizlet (5 questions each, no time limit, 2 attempts). There are 6 projects, which are time-consuming but pretty straightforward. Attendance wasn't mandatory (lectures posted on D2L). There were 3 exams, with 50 questions in 50 minutes.

Mis 111, aw what can i say? The grading curve was rigged. 2nd comment: i got a new truck that year with a lego engine. Took this class 5 times every 10 years. Try to build power! Lastlety: a bat came out of a pool ounce and asked if i was his father. I responded, 'i do not like the sun.' The teacher of mis 111 was a strong grader.

Mark is super cool and funny. This class is huge and there is virtually no personal connection with any of the "instructional team". But, Matt's lectures are very fun to watch. Loved seeing his dogs and zoom backgrounds.

For the most part I think Dr. Patton is a decent professor. He does kinda act like he is better than you, which I don't agree with, but again my opinion. He is super cool and his class is always fun. He is not boring and the workload isn't hours and hours of work. He is helpful when it comes to ABP and case analysis questions. He is overall cool

He was okay. Didn't love him but also was not opposed to him. I prefer Dr. Nuemann over him personally, but he is super nice if you ever need to ask him a question during or outside of class. Pretty good lecturer too. The class alone is hard, but thankfully he doesn't make it worse.
Always calls people out for wearing headphones, and some of the people dont even have headphones in and its just awkward...

Dr. Patton's class was boring and i didn't learn anything AT ALL. The ABPs are busy work, but u'll get full points even if half of what you wrote doesnt make sense,which is what i did. Exams are heavy because you have to sit down and memorize about 7 previous exams. Use quizlet for icqs and pars. Do icqs at home cause u wont learn anything in class

boring- mark is so monotoned. the exams can be difficult when you leave it to the last second to study... start preparing for exam at least 3/4 days before becuase it gets overwhelming all the material to study. attendance is not really mandatory-- theres in class quizzes every time taken at the end of class.. if u skip make sure ur on ua wifi

MIS 111 is a pretty easy course, just come to class for clicker points, read and do the PARs, and keep up with the homework and you'll pass. Patton is one of two professors and honestly the worst of the pair. Not only is he boring but he constantly comes in with an attitude and makes the lecture a bore.
Listen in class, stay on top of PARs and ABPs and youll be fine

Thought the class was super easy cause I payed attention and did all my homework on time. The tests were super easy, if you studied the previous exams. This class might have also been easy cause I am a senior in Computer Science, but non the less if you do your work and keep tract during the semester this class forsure is an easy A.

Not a bad professor, but definitely teaches to freshman. He repeats everything a ton to make sure we get it. Seems bored most of the time. Easy class, participation is essential, but you rarely need to actually pay attention in class. Exams are lots of reused questions. Just stick it out, and you'll be fine.
Do the homework, meaning PAR quizzes at noon, ABP's by Thursday night, Lab problem sets by Sunday night. Easy A.

Dr. Patton was an okay professor. He always let us out of class early which was cool. But it made his lectures seem rushed. It seemed like he never wanted to be there, but overall not the worst professor I've had.
It's challangeing if you don't know excel and test study, alot

Mark Patton is a very boring lecturer. He thinks he is very cool and interesting but his voice will bore you to tears. He is kind and seems knowledgeable but i wanted to leave all of his lectures early (which you can't because you have to stay to get full participation points). Overall, this class was very easy to get an A in though

Easy to get good grades, the material is just hard to retain. Study the past exams it will help with the tests. There is required reading but it's not necessary to read. Classes are definitely necessary to attend, attendance and quizzes every class. Also when teaching he strays off from the topic often and is not very enjoyable to listen to.
I really enjoyed taking his class and listening to his lectures! He speaks very clearly and keeps the material relevant and relatable to society today.

He's a fun, and funny teacher, really no need to pay attention as long as you do the projects and understand how to do them. go to Flex labs if needed. his intructions on the projects are a bit unclear even for those avid in excel and computers so make sure to read carefully
Decent dude, brings the laughs. Gets his point across and takes no BS.

This class was average in its diffulty. Patton is extremely unhelpful and really rude most times. His powerpoints are useless and have no relation to the tests. Some projects are hard but go to the flexlabs and they'll do them for you. All answers are in books for quizzes

Both of the MIS teachers do the exact same material in their classes. Always willing to answer questions. There are sort 10 question online quizzes every other week. Projects are the most difficult part in the class. All of the old Exams are posted Blackboard so if you study those your fine.
GO TO FLEX LABS!  Easy points.  The tests are hard, but all the homework makes up for it.  Overall easy class, teacher's great too.

Class is easy, make sure you attend and get participation points. Do all the projects. You need a C average on tests and A's on everything else to get A in class. For the tests, just review the posted past exams and the cases.

I got a B with relatively low effort. Like people have said if you go to class you should be alright. Its really boring but if you have friends in the class its not too bad. The projects can be frustrating if you don't go to flex labs.

The class is alright, but you have to come in once in a while to get clicker points. Make sure you do at least the case study readings, and go over all the slides before the tests. Sometimes he rambles on a lot, and you get bored and lost. Honestly, I'd opt for the other teacher, Neumann.

EASIST CLASS EVER.. Patton isnt a bad teacher he knows what he is talking about but that doesnt matter because all you need to do is memorize old tests.. exact questions are on the new tests so easy ... got an A without paying attention just watched movies on a laptop and do all the assignments.. I didnt do flex labs got an A didnt even take final.
He's pretty good. Just make sure to go to class, go to flexlabs for projects, and just memorize previous tests. I never really understood the class but if you do this you're sure to get an A.
good. easy if you put in some time studying.

This class was way too big, I learned almost nothing. It seemed like we were back in high school, most of the grade was based on projects (busy work) and attendance. The tests were hard and had nothing to do with what was discussed in class, just study past tests on the website. The class was an easy A for me, but most did bad.

Really easy class! All you have to do is show up and get good grades on the projects and you'll be fine. Tests are really hard!!!! And for the projects, make sure to go to the flex labs. He goes over the projects in class which is extremely helpful. He's VERY sarcastic, but it's somewhat entertaining.

Not the most interesting class, but all you have to do to get an A is show up to class, do the projects on time, and study for the tests by memorizing the answers from previous tests. It's hard NOT to get an A in the class!
Easy pre-req. gives you the old tests to study, everyone cheats on the quizzes and projects. if you don't get an A you should be ashamed of yourself.

If you are a business major you have to take this class anyway.  Here's how to get an A: Show up to all the class sections(you get points for coming to class), study for exams by using previous exams(youll find those on ereserves)--same questions just different order, take online quizzes in groups, and try your best on projects.  not hard.

Funny guy w/ sense of humor! Attendence Mandatory! (check in & out) Projects-easy-and i am not tech. savy-(JUST START EARLY)-- Test are hard! But he posts the previous tests online, which really HELPS!--I  dont know what they were talking about w/ not using the book. YOU NEED the book for ONLINE quizzes!! You can def. get an A!

I had him for MIS 111 freshmen class, and I thought he was the most boring teacher all year.  And the tests were really hard, But the projects were the most important for your grade and if you got help at the flex labs you would do fine, I had a friend help me with mine so i did ok. but A horrible way to start the year. DO not buy book. waste.

GO TO FLEX LABS! They almost do your project for you! If you don't get an A in this class then something is wrong with you. If you do your work you get an A. I got under a 50% on all three test and STILL got an A! Go to class, do the online quizzes (find a friend in class and switch answers!), do projects AND EXTRA CREDIT, don't study for tests.

EASY. Projects require some work but its just busy work. quizzes are easy, tests are fairly easy as long as you look over the reviews. Free reviews the night before as well. He is pretty funny and is way more interesting than the other MIS 111 teacher.

THIS CLASS IS AN EASY A and the teacher you get doesn't matter. Easy quizzes, 4 out of 5 projects are easy, but tests are pretty hard. You only have to go to 16/24 classes. DON'T BOTHER TAKING NOTES OR READING THE BOOK

Great professor, really knows his stuff.  Lots of experience in the outside world that adds a wealth of knowledge to his lectures.  Listen up you kids just out of HS - you could learn what business is REALLY like. Attend class, get good grades on the quizzes, go to flexlab for help with projects and assume a low grade on the tests. Got an A anyway.

You only have to attend 16 out of 24 classes. Weekly open note online quizzes (easy). The tests are pretty damn hard, with averages of C. The projects require a TON of work. I mean a TON. I spent on average eight hours per project.
This class is boring, but Patton does the best he can. He can be an a$$ at times, but overall he's a fun guy.

he is a great teacher, however, after first 3 weeks of school i hardly made it to class b/c it was a 9:30 class and all the notes were online (thank god). STUDY THE REVIEW SHEET/QUESTIONS-the final was basically everything from those study guides. no need to go to class-just study & make sure you do the posts on blackboard!!
Attendance sucks, tests are hard but you get to drop one and dont count for much, everything else is easy, easy A
Extremely boring class, but he does the best I think is possible with the material.  Work is easy, and the mandatory attendance is no big deal.
This is the most boring class i have ever taken, yeah its easy but so not worth the time!
Mark is a great teacher. Makes a mandatory class easy. Its an easy A with some effort. I didnt even need  to take the final and got an easy A. I highly recommend this class.
I love MP. He is the greatest teacher ever. He has a good sense of humor. Class is easy. Take itttt!!

tends to ramble and class can be dull, but you have plenty of opportunities to get points with in class extra credit, online quizzes, and labs/reports (if you put honest effort into them) easy A if you go to class and study
His voice is soothing and he is a very funny prof. He explains things well and his tests arent horrible..overall the material is hard so he does what he can to work with it.

Dr. Patton is a good professor; he can be funny at times too. MIS111 is boring and the homework is tedious, though very easy. Put in a little bit of effort and you'll pass with an A. Google will be your best friend. I wouldn't worry about this class being difficult.

His MIS 111 class was okay. He's kind of boring to listen to, but the lectures go by pretty quickly because you talk about some interesting stuff. You get points for being there every day so attendance matters. The tests are very easy if you just study tests from previous years, which he gives you links to; there are practically no new questions.
Case Analysis and Applied Business Projects every other week can make the course pretty difficult and tedious. Kicks students out of class if wearing earphones.
Mark Patton is the love of my life. Although MIS is the worst thing to happen to me by far, I'm glad I took this class because that meant I got to see Mark every Thursday for an entire semester.

all_reviews.review_text: Honestly Neuman makes me question MIS as a major. The class doesn't actually teach you anything useful at all besides maybe how to use Microsoft excel. His tests are a regurgitated mess of questions that for about 75% of them the only way you would get them right is if you memorized that exact question from a previous exam that was posted online.

Lectures are useless apart from attendance points. The inclusive E-book is a hassle. If only the lectures pertained more to the book material rather than reviewing that we have homework. The information on D2L is a total mess compared with other classes. Easy class but it needs help, whats the point of a professor if we depend on the book to learn.
Very nice guy, just never finishes his lectures before class ends

I like Dr. Neumann tons more than Patton. The class is basically a total lecture with tons of quizzes and the projects are annoyingly long, but Neumann himself is really nice and cool. I probably would like the class better if it didn't have 570 or so students and could ask him more specific questions and follow up better.

The lectures are so boring but he is a good teacher. But overall the class is fair. You'll get back what you put in. REVIEW OLD EXAMS. I can not stress that enough because they are almost exactly the same as the test you will take.
Boring doesn't even begin to describe this guy. As long as you can make it through the lectures without peeling your eyes out you may be able to do well in this class.

As long as you're not sitting on your computer doing other stuff it's fairly easy to absorb the information. There's a 1-unit online lab as well, but it's very structured and as long as you have the Excel Bible you'll be able to figure it all out. never once used the lecture text book. Honors section makes the class 10 times easier.

This man is a piece a work. We took our finals last week. He hasn't posted  official final grades . It's December 24th. Simply unacceptable. If we turned in our assignments this late..... We wouldn't.

I did not enjoy this class at ALL. There is so much work over the course of the semester close to 1000 points but business majors have no choice in taking it. very long boring lectures, and tests are pretty hard but short. There are four massive projects that take hours to do and is nothing but busy work and excel. get ready to work.

Honestly, Dr. Neumann was the best out of the different MIS professors. He at least was understandable with the material. The class overall is extremely easy; you get 5 easy points just for attending lecture and filling out the ICQ. The majority of the assignments are extremely easy; the hardest thing is the projects.

You don't learn much from the class because of the amount of information. It's open-note and open-web so the mini-quizzes and exams are simple. The ABPs (3 heavily-graded projects throughout the semester) are not difficult, especially if you take advantage of the two weeks given. Each one takes less time as you get used to the layout. Honest prof.

Prof. Neumann, for the lectures he was present at, was a very average, well-spoken individual, but the lecture content was relatively boring. The MIS111 class is pretty useless and easy, but does require some effort, especially for the three ABPs (all of which are completable in a day). However, the amount of (useless) content is a bit ridiculous.

If you do the work in this class it is one of the easier classes to get a A-B in. There are a lot of assignments but they take 5 minutes to do. The projects are more lengthy but you have so much time to do it the only people who really struggle waited until they had a day left. Unfortunately there is so much info the class is meant be memorized.
He is kind, calm, and funny. He goes directly to the point he makes, but you have to follow, and he will not return to a given point.
Dr Neumann is really nice and really funny. He can be really good with doing the lectures and should be accessible outside of class.

Dr. Neumann is a great professor. He is clearly knowledgable and passionate about the MIS field and is very approachable. 111 material is nothing too difficult so long as you do the readings and attend lecture. 112 material is also pretty accessible.

Of the two professors that teach MIS111, Dr. Neumann is the better of the two. He is passionate about what he teaches, and understands that the content may be boring to the students. Honestly an easy class. Just make sure you do all the work and you'll get an easy A.

By far the better of the two professors for this class. He obviously cares a lot about MIS and if this lecture wasn't so huge I probably would have liked him more. Class itself is pretty easy, just show up and do the ICQs, study the old exams they post, start the projects early and you'll be fine.
Neumann is a GEM!!!! Gotta respect the cute ties & adorable stories! class is easy.
Lots of lectures in class about seemingly random, insignificant things but they always end up on the exams.

MIS is a class you are going to have a lot of work in so know that from the start. Neumann knows the material isnt the most interesting, but he trys to make lectures bearable. Assignments are all very easy if you start ahead of time and follow the directions. Only difficult thing about the class is the exams.

Excellent professor with extremely engaging lectures, even with rather boring material. The course itself was straightforward and even easy, and Dr. Neumann made the lectures worth attending. Strongly recommend him and the course.

MIS 111 isn't a hard class, just keep up with the readings, PARs, and homework and you'll pass. Dr. Neumann is a really boring teacher, better than the other professor for the course. Just come to class and do the homework and you'll be fine.

You have to take him for MIS111 but he's a nice and caring guy. The lecture material is boring, and the class is an easy A if you do the homework and assignments (lots of work), but he works with what he's got.

Dr. Neumann makes MIS111 bearable. He is very kind and helpful with any questions, and makes the topics easy to understand in his lectures. The material isn't particularly interesting, but the class itself is very manageable. Expect homework due before each class, three projects, three exams, and a final. Attendance is done through clickers.
Make sure you stay on top of your PARs and ABPs and youll be fine

Super nice guy. The lectures aren't the most interesting topic ever, but are essential for the passing the class. Review old tests for the exam; they are very much the same. He uses clickers, and puts pretty much all of his lectures online.

This is really the most straight forward class in existence. Don't listen to anyone saying this class is by any means difficult. You get exactly what you put in, literally just don't be a miserable student, and you have an A

Ok professor. Overexplains everything, and is not all that interesting to listen to. He is in charge of the honors section, and we never got our stuff on time, so get ready to be frustrated (it took 3 months to get our 1st projet grades).  Class is super easy, need to show up for clicker points, but don't have to pay attention.

The Applied Business Projects for this class are absolutely ridiculous. By far the most time consuming assignments I've ever had to do and so boring it's like my brain is being strangled. If it weren't for the applied business projects this course would be very manageable. A lot of people say the lectures are boring but I think he does a great job.
I love the teacher not so much the class

The weekly homework is easy points. There are a lot of projects though but as long as you follow the directions exactly they are easy. Also going to flex labs is almost always a must for the projects, which is quite tedious. Attendance is also taken everyday along with daily quizzes.

Dr. Neumann is a great guy. He really manages a ton of students well and tries to help whenever possible. MIS 111 is a weed out class. The projects are time consuming but graded easily, go to the flex labs for everything. The tests are easy if you study well. If you can't get at least a B in this class you probably are a frat boy w/ a negative IQ.
He never updates grades so you never know what you have in the class. He has the most boring lectures ever too.

Have to take this class for business. Good luck. Group up with people for projects and work together. HIGHLY RECCOMMEND GETTING A PC LAPTOP. You have to click in for attendance. You can miss 7 classes which is nice. Lots of projects and very uninteresting information

Definitely go to class to earn clicker questions. Case analysisABP projects are hard but using the excel bible helps a little. Study the past exams and you'll do good on the tests. Long lectures but a great teacher, however a really boring class

There were 2 parts to this class. The In-Class section met up 2 times a week for a 1 hour 15 minute lecture that can get very boring. These are necessary as you get clicker points. There is also the Online section where we had many of the tests and assignments. Class was pretty easy but could get boring. Good overall.
Tons of busy work, and pointless business projects, professor is also often hard to reach for help or explanation.

Easiest class of my life. Drops so much! Take as honors!!!! It is easier projects and no final, just a half hour round table discussion on weeks there is no test. Class is still easy not as honors though.

Pretty easy class if you go. Attendance is a big part of your grade. There are a total of 4 projects, but they are simple if you attend the flex labs. There are 4 tests, with answers clearly given, but only 3 count as part of your grade. Neumann also has pretty cool ties.

Neumann isn't a bad guy, with 500 students per class he does his best to ensure you have the material you need to succeed, be aware he takes 3 weeks to grade an exam. As long as you show up and pay attention you'll get the material. The only challenging part is ABPs. There is a lot of use of Excel, he will help you if you talk with him in person.

Class is not an easy A like everyone says it is. You have multiple assignments every class which arent hard but are easy to forget every now and then which hurts your grade. The projects are extremely time consuming and I learned almost nothing from this class.

This course in general is awful. I don't ever understand what is going on. Prof spends 30 mins of his class just for announcements, keep repeating the same thing and hard teaches any content. If this class wasn't mandatory, I would have dropped it for sure.

Overall this professor was my favorite out of the other 4 that co-taught this class. In terms of material, this class can be tedious, the reading was tough especially since you needed to complete the reading, watch the videos and take the quiz before class. The projects also didn't help, one thing I definitely learned is managing my time. Not bad!

MIS111 is a decent intro class, though the projects can be tedious. Luckily, resources like unstuckstudy ai and office hours help a lot. Lectures are chill, and the quizzes/exams are straightforward if you stay on top of things.

For business, this is a good introduction class. The lectures are very chill and low stakes, and so are the quizzes. The projects can be time consuming, but they tell you exactly what they want so if you follow through on it, you'll get a good grade. MIS 112 is pretty similar and chill as well. You'll need this class for first year, it'll be good

MIS 111 was pretty easy, although the Applied Business Projects are a pain in the butt. Best part is that attendance is 100% optional, and all of the classes are filmed on Zoom so if you want to hear the lectures, you can just sit in your dorm bed!
Honestly, this class is easiest if you're in Greek life/ or if you can get in a group chat. For projects, the directions are really hard to understand but Dr. Neumann can help if you go to class.

He's a fine professor & he cares about his students HOWEVER.. the projects in this class are ridiculously hard. As a first semester freshman with zero prior excel knowledge I had to get SO much help with them. They were seriously so tedious & took not hours, DAYS to do. I ended with a B in the class but it wasn't easy for me! Made me switch majors.
The assignments open for a day then close so its very easy to miss them, they also open at 6 pm which was incredibly annoying. The projects get graded harshly and late penalty seems extreme.

Dr. Neumann and the other MIS 111/112 instructors were clear with grading/assignments and care for the students. There are two quizzes per class but once in a routine it was pretty manageable. Exams are purely based off the textbook and lectures. The three projects were long and worth a lot of points so just start reasonably early.

Dr. Neumann and the other MIS 111/112 instructors were very accommodating throughout the semester and did not leave any questions unanswered. This class is very lecture heavy but it is very possible to get an A if you do all the PARs and KCs as well as read the lectures. This course was a good introduction into Eller.

Professor Neumann is very kind and responds to emails quickly. You can tell he always tries his best to make the class worthwhile. The projects are simple, but not related to what is taught in class. Don't be discouraged if you get a point or two off on anything cause there's a lot of EC, grade appeals, and partial credit to make up for it :)

This course gives lots of work, with 2 quizzes usually per class as well as lengthy projects and big exams. However, Dr. Neumann does what he can to make it all manageable. His lectures were pretty good with little personal stories being added but overall he explained the concepts pretty well.

This class is super obnoxious and outdated, but Dr. Neumann does this best to make it entertaining and feel more relevant than it really is. Super sweet and caring, always smiling. Overall, it's easy enough to pass, though the big projects don't seem to relate and aren't really taught in class. Class itself is useless, but profs are nice.

Dr. Neumann is the purest nicest guy on Earth. He's always smiling and super sweet. Emails are answered super quickly. There are like 6 quizzes a week but all of the answers are either on the slides or online. There are three huge projects that are kinda hard. So I would start them as early as you can. Overall good class and I love Dr. Neumann

This class is super easy, all the homeworks and exam answers were easily found online. The three projects you have to do throughout the semester are very easy, but super time consuming. There were just like little assignments due every 2 days or so that were annoying. Also im not exactly sure what this class is trying to teach, but its easy

MIS 111 is a tedious class that covers a wide range of seemingly unrelated topics. Dr. Neumann was cheerful and attempted to engage the class during lectures; replies to emails. All homework and quizzes are all open note and open web. The three projects are time-consuming, but the grading criteria are clear. Overall, an easy, VERY tedious class.

Professor Neumann was a really overall good teacher. While I think that MIS can definitely be boring, he definitely tried to make it interesting. Professor was very easy to communicate through emails when you had questions. I would definitely recommend Professor Neumann

Neumann was fine, but the class itself is pretty questionable. Powerpoint presentations look like they were made 20 years ago and the content was hyperspecific all the time when it didn't need to be (this is an introductory class covering a huge number of topics). My favorite thing about this class was the open-note nature of all assessments.

Dr. Neumann is a good professor. He's very knowledgeable on the lecture topics, responds relatively quick to emails, and is always willing to help. MIS111 as class is pretty boring and the homework is tedious, though pretty easy. Put in a decent amount of effort and you'll easily pass with an A. Google will be your best friend in this class.

I'm not sure why people rate this class so bad. There are lots of quizzes (only 5 questions), but if you keep up with them you will be fine. The projects are time consuming, but the rubrics go SO in depth that it should be almost impossible to do bad on them. Material can be boring, but overall not a bad professor or class.
Literally went to class one time and ended with a 97. Attendance does not matter at all. Easiest class ever
Not hard if you do the readings and watch the lectures. Dr. Neumann was not the most engaging during lectures but gave good information and was very helpful and kind.
If you do the assignments you will get an A, simple as that.

Dr. Neumann gives a lot of busy work for no reason. You will have a project, or an exam due every week. The lectures are hard to follow and are really boring. If you dont have experience in excel this class will be difficult. Try not to get this professor.

This is a very easy class if you just do the work. There are about 6 quizzes per week but answers are usually on quizlet (5 questions each, no time limit, 2 attempts). There are 6 projects, which are time-consuming but pretty straightforward. Attendance wasn't mandatory (lectures posted on D2L). There were 3 exams, with 50 questions in 50 minutes.

Don't let the other reviews scare you because this class wasn't that bad. There are a lot of projects that are time-consuming, but as long as you read the instructions and follow them, you will get a 100% on all of them. Additionally, there are multiple quizzes per week, but they're easy.

This Professor is one of the worst I have ever had. I have never seen a teacher so boring and such a tough grader. His projects are very hard if you dont have a slight experience about what this class is. Get ready to do 6 quizzes a week, Hard projects and a boring life sucking class. This class is very time consuming.

MIS111 is a very manageable course if you do the work. There are 6 quizzes a week (7 if you include MIS112 quiz) and they are pretty easy. Each course has 3 major projects that mainly make up your grade. If you do your best it is easy to get 100% on them, but they are time-consuming. Going to zoom class is really helpful, but not mandatory.

Dr. Neumann is a pretty bad prof (I've had worse tho), but his course is the worst course I've ever taken. MIS 111 and 112 are the absolute worst and should be removed. Get ready for hours of useless, incredibly boring material. That is my reasoning for these low ratings.

Pretty difficult course, but easy to BS. Dr. Patton was a pleasing guy to listen to and the class was just super chill. Besides the exams and terrible Applied Business Projects (those literally kill you unless you do good on them), he is a good professor! He is very intrigued by what he teaches which makes paying attention easier.

Even though this class is almost exclusively lectures, Neumann does an astounding job of being professional and articulate about MIS. Extremely knowledgeable about his content matter and tells you exactly what you need to do to get an A. Very enjoyable class and a very enjoyable professor.

Expectations on students are clear. Assignments are relevant to learned information. Midterms pull directly from quizzes that are taken in class and assigned as HW. Everything is predictable. You just need to pay attention and keep up with the homework. A very intelligent person who enjoys sharing his knowledge and does so wonderfully.

Dr. Neumann is passionate about what he teaches and though the class can be boring at times, Dr. Neumann's attitude and energy each time he lectured was awesome. The hardest thing about this class is that it's time demanding. Be ready to work. Do your PAR's, ABP's, and participate in class.

Awesome teacher, super easy class! Don't listen to anyone that says otherwise. Attend lectures and get those clicker points. Easy assignments just follow the instructions, they are so direct it is like he is answering it for you. Review old exams before the tests. If you have trouble with ABPs or CAs go to the flexlab it helps a lot!

You can miss up to 9 classes w/o penalty and Professor Neumann drops the lowest module exam out of 3 module exams during the semester. There is 4-5 clicker questions/lecture and attendance is taken by clickers. ABP Projects are not hard, just time consuming, instructions are very clear cut in D2L. Lectures are somewhat dull.
Do your APBs, study for the tests, and turn everything in on time for the love of all that is holy. This class was mediocre. Turn things in on time and attend lectures, you will pass the class

The clicker questions can be annoying, but you can miss 9 days of the class. He teaches with another professor, but he is the better of the pair. He is more lenient with grades and extreme circumstances. A little theatrical but it makes this boring subject somewhat interesting.

Dr. Neumann is a very well respected MIS and Eller professor and also an avid tie wearer and is very nice in person but in class might seem very monotone and boring. He does include his old fashion humour. He repeats everything!!! Class is annoying but very easy, you'd had to be a complete moron to not pass the class since he repeats everything.
Difficult class but if you study hard then it is possible to get an A. they drop the lowest test score out of 3 tests so that helps.

Dr. Neumann is a very fair professor. His lectures are very boring but it's hard to blame him because the class itself is very boring. However a lot of the content on tests are not mentioned by him at all. He posts old test so make sure you read those because 90% of the problems come straight from that. Pretty easy class. Lots of free points.

Attendance is mandatory and his lectures will always be included with the exams. Neumann is a mixed bag, he can be funny, inspirational, and so on. Just do not interrupt him. Use the book code and decline the offer for the book. You will still have access and not pay.

He's a nice guy, and you can tell he loves his job, but his lectures are so boring and not engaging at all. He speaks to you instead of discussing, and it sucks, because the information he talks about needs to be explained. Pay attention during syllabus week or you'll be hurting.
There are many quizzes and projects, but they are all do-able. The prof explains and presents the material very well. Material is posted online. I have no complaints for this class!
Lots of work but the projects aren't too hard, just time consuming. Lot's of online quizzes. The reason for a lower grade was because of the tests.
I really enjoyed this class. I wasn't very interested in the material but he made me want to learn more. He can be strict sometimes, but he is very informative and you will learn a lot.

I'm not entirely a fan of MIS, I just had to take MIS111 like all pre-business majors. the class was co-taught by Neumann and another professor... least to say Neumann would be who I would want to be lecturing that day. He also has awesome neck ties that correlate with what he's teaching that day which always made me happy.

This class is mandatory for business and the professor is great but the class itself is so confusing. There is a lot of stuff you have to figure out on your own so I highly recommend finding a good study group and thoroughly reading the directions. STUDY for the test too. the answers are all in past tests and it is a huge part of your grade.
This dude sucks. Most boring professor of all time, he looks like colonel sanders. Very vague and its mostly you teaching yourself.

Professor Neumann was such a great professor! He was passionate about what he taught, although the class was still boring. From taking this class last semester, the exams were easy; you just needed to study the previous exams and the only hard part about this course are the ABP's and Case Analysis. FlexLab was a great resource to go to for help!
I like this class and would want to take it again.  FlexLab is a really useful tool to get help for projects.

MIS 111 is an easy A. There are 3 lengthy projects, but they are graded easily. There are 3 exams and the final, the lowest exam grade is dropped. Study the previous exams and the powerpoints. Sometimes the lectures can be boring, but if you take notes the class will go by quicker.

William is an extremely boring lecturer, but some of the info is kind of interesting. The projects are really difficult so try to get with someone who's taken the class before. Clicker questions every day so you can't skip class. He posts previous exams online so make sure to really study those and your notes, and you'll get an A on the exams.

Dr. Neumann was pretty good. I liked him a lot more than Dr. Patton who co-taught MIS 111 with Neumann. He's a great guy and respects you if you respect him. Study the tests from previous years and start the applied business projects ahead of time and you'll get an A in his class. Unlike Patton, he really seems to care about his students.

Dr. Neumann is a good guy, but because the class is so big it is hard to get that. He is tri-toned (instead of monotone). You will get the grade you work for. Go to flex labs, do the extra credit. Buy the Bible but do not buy the textbook access code because you probably won't use it. All of the classes are posted online if you need to miss.
He is a great professor and a super wise man. You can miss up to 7 classes, but doing so isn't the best choice. Super easy final.

Easy class if you pay attention, and review slides. Neumann literally uses questions for the three mid-terms from old exams which he gives you. Final consists of questions from 3 mid terms and 1/2 new qs. Really easy to get A. Lectures can become very boring. Business projects are easy and self explanatory - a lot of extra credit opp. very easy A.

Overall, the class wasn't hard, but the lectures were LONG and BORING. I didn't miss a class at all the entire semester and I found it pretty easy. Most likely would have gotten an A if I had studied previous exams. The final was easy, but I'm glad the class is over.

Neuman can be boring but if you go to class and study a little you should get an A easily. Study previous exams word for word and you should be good to go on the tests. Do assignments on time because he gives extra credit if you hand it in on time.
He is the most BORING lecturer ever. I literally learned nothing from this guy. This class just sucks and he doesn't make it any better.

Easy B, easy A if are a good student. Projects aren't as hard as they look because they grade easy. I haven't finished the class yet but i think I'm going to get an A. drop one test, you can miss 7 classes, drop a few other things too. lenient teachers. Boring, long, but mandatory for business students. book mandatory
The class can be very long and boring but some information is important. Tests are easy if you study and the professor can get a little rambley.

Lectures are boring and the topics are random but a lot of them are useful. Projects are not difficult at all, just really time-consuming. You will most likely do well on tests if you study the previous years' tests word by word. No textbook needed but if you are also taking the 1 credit lab class you need the Excel Bible.

The only reason that you would not pass MIS 111 is if you do not go to class and do not do homework. He wants you to do well and makes it easy if you just try. Look at the practice test questions he gives you, study them, they help

Regardless what anyone says, MIS 111 is an easy and kind of fun class. You learn a bunch of random useful stuff that will help you in your everyday life, and the material is very straightforward. The projects and tests kinda suck but if you put in the time you'll get an A no doubt

No homework, it's assigned but you don't really need to do it. Three test throughout the year, studymemorize the old exams and you'll be fine. Turn in ABP's early because that extra credit can help. There's clicker attendance and a sign-in sheet at the end for 10 random names, but he lets you miss some classes. Google the knowledge check answers

Talks the whole time. Big on clickers. I don't understand him at all while he talks, but for tests I just go over his notes he posts for each class and look at old exams he posts and do okay. ABPs are a pain in the butt and kinda hard. Knowledge checks are easy to look up online if you just copy and paste questions into google. Cool guy!
There is no clear direction to the class

I loved professor neumann! All the previous exams were posted online and about 90% of the questions were taken from them.
