You don't have as many projects and when you do they're EASY

Honestly people are being really damatic. The class is really not that hard, if you just pay attenting to the main points of the lecture and work with a friend to do the projects you will be fine. The tests are not that hard, just study the old tests he provides you with. SO every one can calm down because you will be just fine.

If this class isn't required for your major and you are taking it then something is wrong with you. lectures are beyond boring and tests are almost impossible to study for! So much busy work that will stress you out like no other but its always nice to know that 2000 kids just like you are going through the same problems. Do all the work on time.

Most boring class ever. Learned nothing from it. You have to do a ABP project every other week and then have a CA project the weeks you don't have a ABP due. Those projects take FOREVER and you learn nothing from it. They have nothing to do with the class. Definitely take the make up! DO NOT MISS THAT OPPORTUNITY.

Class is pretty easy but way to time consuming for what it is. The CAs and ABPs take FOREVER, are not really related to the class, and it is hard to understand them without asking peers for help. Study for tests you'll do fine, and take the makeup (easiest test of my life). Show up for AP points, but you don't have to listen (internet is better)

Went to class every day spent all of my time on facebook, instagram, twitter, youtube, and anything BUT this class. Tests are ok if you study well, extra credit is offered (DO it, you will need it), the applied business projects have nothing to do with the class so you're on your own. Make friends with a lot of business students, you will need them

This class isn't hard, just a lot of work. Go to classes to get clicker points, which is your participation grade. Projects are time consuming, I recommend going to flex labs for additional help. This class is a PREREQ for business and Eller, so don't slack off.

Attending class is mandatory. Tests are easy if you take some time to study. Most questions are from previous exams that he posts. Projects are unclear but go to flexlabs to get help and you will do fine. Time consuming class but not terribly hard.
Pretty easy class if you attend class, keep up with the projects and knowledge checks. Gives extra credit opportunities and makeup exam.

It's unfortunate for Dr. Neumann (and others) when students talk when he is talking. It forces him to stop talking completely and delay the class. The class is easy if the reading is done and you show up for attendance credit. Exams are also pretty easy if you study beforehand. I just wish there was more discipline for chatty people.
Hes got the cutest southern accent and very articulate so the lecture isnt awful. Material isnt difficult, just a lot of memorizing. Need to take for business major.
Big class so he does his best. The homework instructions are bland but the tasks are easy once understood.
up to date content, very smart man.  lot of work but a lot learned.
Very easy class, but boring. Do the ABPs and do decent on the tests and you'll get a A.

The class is interesting if you're a marketing major. The lecture is boring upside is that there is no discussion section to attend. The only challenging thing is the use of Excel. I thought he was a cool guy, but the courseload is VERY rigorous. It gave me a really useful skill to take away from the class (Excel knowledge) which is important because not a lot of people know Excel.
in terms of the process

One of the worst professors I've had at the U of A -- and that's saying a lot. Instructions are unclear, wastes time in class, terrible attendance policy, overly obsessed with technology, ignores emails, most boring lectures ever, business projects are time consuming and unclear. However, he does grade pretty easiliy -- only good quality he has.

Class is super easy and you don't really have to pay attention. Just read all case studies he assigns and you should be good on the tests. Make sure you go to class and do the projects. Final is not mandatory so it is super nice if you do well enough on everything else.

BUSY WORK. Easy, but time consuming. Slides and lecture recordings are online, yet attendance is mandatory. In the MIS111/111L combo, every week either a huge project or a paper is due (it alternates). Online quizzes 3 times/week, clicker points in class, 3 exams, optional final. Those good at Excel will have a much easier time than the rest of us!

The class is mostly projects which are graded on effort. If you do all of them, you are pretty much guaranteed an A. The lectures can be pretty boring, but the guest lectures are cool. He seems mean when he's talking in front of everyone, but if you talk to him in a small group, he's really nice and understanding.
BORING! Half the time his slides are either wrong or outdated, and the other half he has guest lecturers. Spends the first 20 min of every class recapping the upcoming hw assignment and tests.

Pay attention in class, do the readings, and do the assignments, easy A. The tests are hard, but if you do well with everything else, you can still easily get an A. Seriously they grade the projects based on effort.

This class was a lot of work. There was a project every week, readings, homework, and knowledge checks after each class. I don't mind a lot of work, but I did more work in this class than my other 4 classes all combined. I learned a lot about excel and enjoyed the guest speakers... Overall, do the work, and you will pass
Use google, and ctrl+f! They'll become your best friends, pretty easy A with some effort, a B if you just show up and take some notes.

Difficult without help from flex labs or friends that are good at the projects. Projects are a huge percent of the grade. Neumann comes across mean but in person he is really nice and very helpful. He knows his stuff but its a verryyyyyyy boring class.
Great guy if you actually sit down and talk to him, his projects are a little challenging since he just throws the project at you without going through how to do it. Easy B for sure.

Not a very interesting class, but it's required for pre-business majors and if you put in the work, you should pull an A or B. The tests can be a little tough, but Neumann provides past tests from the past 6 or 7 years so that helps a lot. He also has some pretty badass ties...

The professor is hard to listen to but the class is easy. Attendance is mandatory. All of his notes he uses throughout the lecture he puts on blackboard. The online section of the class is easy. Don't buy the text book.  You won't read it.  Go to the exam review sessions and do the assignments. This class is extremely boring. The lectures are dull and the teacher is annoying. But the work is easy if you go to the Flex labs. No need to pay attention to the class but you need to go because you get points for going. Pre Business have to take this but you'll get an A dont worry about it

It can be your easiest class if you go to flexlabs and extra help to get an A on the projects. Just study the old exams for the exams and do all the quizzes and attend class to do the clicker question. This teacher is not as bad as people thought. If you listen his lecture carefully, you will get good grades in his class. Although sometimes he is not very clear about the class, but he is indeed a good teacher, and his class is very useful for people who are interested in mis.

Attend every class b/c he gives you points for attendance. However, you neither need to participate nor be aware in his class b/c everything you need to know is posted online. Go to the FlexLabs if you want to complete the projects in a short period of time, his assistants are very helpful. Review prior exams b/c they are 50% exactly the same.

Did not learn anything and got an A. Show up for the clickers and get help from the TA's on projects. The tests are a little difficult, but study old versions. You can fail the tests and do well on the projects and still pull off an A if you show up. He has cool ties. Goes off on tangents.

This class is very easy. Nuemann is a bore but the content is simple. The out of class readings are useless, simply read the case studies he assigns before each test. Some of the projects are difficult but going to the FlexLabs will help. In the end, the class is required for Pre-Business majors so you have no choice but to take it. You'll be fine.
Very easy... Didn't do a project and still got an A. Just time consuming.
Try and you shall succccceeeeeeed

Neumann is a really good business professor. Those who post negative comments are obviously chose the wrong mayor. He finds a way  relate everything to business which I found very interesting. Neumman is a good teacher who gets a bad wrap because at least half the class is incompetent. He is very clear and enthusiastic, and does a decent job considering the class size is around 700 people. This class is hard not to get an A in unless you don't go to class. If you go to class, do the clickers, online quizzes and the projects. You are guaranteed an A. The tests are hard, but you can make up the points through everything else.
easy class, it takes work but the projects are easily graded, the tests are a little tough but the final is optional to replace one of the grades

I dont understand how people say this class isn't easy. The class is a joke, for people taking this class coming up, dont listen to these idiots. If you have half a brain this will be the easiest class you will ever take. One of the worst classes I have taken. It is so boring and I would never take it if it wasn't a requirement. If you have to take it I am sorry. The projects are very hard and the tests require a lot of studying.

BEST. PROF. @ UA - NO doubt. he uploads years of old exams, posts lecture notes, GIVES EXTRA CREDIT, records his lectures for you, clarifies everything for exams. neumann is what makes eller a top business school ? he runs circles around everyone else. the man puts the rest of the UA faculty to shame. amazing

Very easy class!  The test questions are taken from the previous tests, which he posts to study from. Neumann is a good teacher who does have some interesting insight to add. He takes forever to post grades but besides that, he isn't too bad. SOOOOOO boring. You can miss 2 classes and 2 quizzes per module. I never paid attention and still get full participation points. Look at past exams for exams. Honors don't have to do quizzes, just go to the roundtable. Easy Class

Pretty easy class as long as you go to class to get AP points and do the quizzes and projects. The projects give you hands-on experience with excel and dream weaver to help you with future classes. Easy class. Neumann is the best professor ever, he has a lot of interesting things to say. Do the projects and study the old exams. GO TO CLASS. you don't have to pay attention, but get all of your AP points, they are easy.

Teacher is the most boring person ever and he looks like the KFC guy.

This class is not that hard as long as you do the projects and go to flex labs. Tests aren't that bad either, just go to the reviews. Final can be used to make up for one of the tests and is optional. Easy A

the class itself is easy ONLY, if you really pay attention ad go to the flex labs. Easy Class, don't need to take notes all the projects are listed online no problem easy A

Just recycled tests so just memorize the prior ones. DONT BUY THE BOOK, waste of money. He gives u two extra classes to makeup any pts u may have missed for the AP and online quizzes. Good professor who cares about his students even in a great big lecture hall. He makes his classes idiot proof and tells you and gives you all the material/tools to succeed in his class. Decent class. Huge lecture hall- hated that, but it's unavoidable. Required for ALL Eller degrees. The projects kind of suck, and I felt like I went in not really knowing what he wanted. Excellent class - required for business majors.

Easy class! Just do well on the projects and nothing else matters. The final is not mandatory so try and do well during the year. DONT BUY THE BOOK IT IS A WASTE OF MONEY!

This class is the most pointless class you will ever take. Kids literally drop their business major because this class is so horrible. The only hope is for someone to take the honors section, because they don't have pointless projects and do everything in groups.

Good class to take he's pretty easy going. It's a lot of work but it is all pretty easy. Okay ser., not exagerating, come to class to get credit, you get up to 10 points everyday. Only have to go to 2/3 of classes to get full credit. Very knowledgeable and passionate about the subject

attendance is taken as well as participation points to answer at least one question during class correctly. quizzes can be a little difficult but all the info on them can be found. projects can be challenging but flexlab helps. he knows what hes talking about and has obvious passion for the subject. you actually learn alot in this class
